node.data: Parent theory for N=2 chiral-planar mirror dualities
Obtained by real mass deformation of N=4 U(N) SQCD
Obtained by ungauging the diagonal U(1) of U(N) SQCD
Self-mirror SCFT with global symmetry SU(N)xSU(N)xU(1).
U(N) CS-SQCD with [F1+N, F2+N] chiral multiplets
SQCD theory with fundamental and anti-fundamental matter
U(N) SQCD with [F1+N, F2+N] chiral multiplets
SQCD theory with bifundamental and flavor blocks
U(N) SQCD with [F1+2N, F2] chiral multiplets
Confines to a single free chiral multiplet
N=4 U(2N) SQCD with F hypermultiplets and rank-2 antisymmetric tensor
N=2 U(2N) SQCD with F fundamentals and anti-chiral antisymmetric field
Derived from N=4 theory via mass deformation
U(2N)_(0,-3N) CS-SQCD3 with F fundamentals and rank-2 antisymmetric tensor
U(1) theory with F fundamental multiplets
Result of Higgsing USp(2) to U(1)
U(1) QED3 with Ns scalars and Nf fermions
Theory with 5 fundamental scalars and fermions.
SU(N) QCD3 with Ns scalars and Nf fermions
General proposal for planar Abelian dual
SU(3) at level -1/2 with 6 scalars and 1 fermion
SU(2) at level -5/2 with 5 scalars and 1 fermion
U(N) QCD3 with Ns scalars and Nf fermions
SU(3) QCD3 with Ns scalars and Nf fermions
SU(3)_0 QCD3 with 5 scalars and 2 fermions
SU(2)_1/2 QCD3 with 2 scalars and 3 fermions
u n k f fund zone1
Planar Abelian Mirror of U(N) SQCD
Obtained by Higgsing N=4 mirror nodes to Cartan subgroup
Planar Abelian Mirror of SU(N) SQCD

Linear quiver UV completion of FT[U(N)].

Planar Abelian dual of U(N)_k CS-YM
Planar Abelian dual of U(3)_0 with 8 fundamentals
Planar Abelian dual of U(3)_{-1/2} with 7 fundamentals

Organized into columns of U(1) gauge nodes

Linear quiver with alternating orthogonal and symplectic gauge groups

Mirror theory for SU(5) flavor symmetry theory.
Planar mirror of SU(2)_-2 with 5 scalars
Dual theory for SU(2)_-2 QCD3 with 5 scalars.
Planar Abelian dual of SU(N) QCD3
Planar Abelian dual of SU(3) at level -1/2
Planar Abelian dual of SU(2) at level -5/2

Dual to SU(2) QCD3 with F scalars

Gauge-invariant generator of the chiral ring
Generated by contracting F diagonal bifundamental fields
Mapped to monopole operator with flux +1
dual to a gauge invariant monopole
Linear monopole superpotential for adjacent nodes
Dual to gauge-invariant monopole operator M
spin-1 mode of one fermion, spin-0 mode of other

edge.data: Higgsing of mirror nodes to Cartan subgroup
Higgsing each U(k) node to Cartan subgroup

Step 1: The quiver is chopped into its basic QFT blocks. Step 2: Each QFT block is dualized via the basic duality moves. Step 3: The dualized blocks are glued together via the N=2 chiral/planar fusion to identity.
decomposition into fundamental blocks, dualization, gluing via identity walls
SU(F) x U(1)_s x U(1)_eta x U(1)_tau
Higgsing each U(k) gauge node to maximal torus U(1)^k
Higgsing each U(k) gauge node to its maximal torus U(1)^k
Higgsing bottom-left diagonal, chiral field connecting bottom-most gauge and flavor nodes acquires positive mass
mirror loses one node from left or right column
large FI limit for the rightmost node
large FI limit for the leftmost node
mirror loses one column of fermions
mirror loses one column of bosons
SU(F) x U(1)_s x U(1)_eta x U(1)_tau
R-charge computation and chiral ring matching
